markets.question: New Rihanna Album before GTA VI?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Rihanna officially releases a new album before Grand Theft Auto VI is officially released in the US. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If neither occurs by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source for the release of GTA VI is official information from Rockstar Games or its parent company, Take-Two Interactive.

Officially released means that a newly released album is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date.

Re-releases, deluxe editions, compilations, or greatest hits albums will not count, unless they contain at least 50% tracks that have never been officially released in any format prior to the album's release.

The resolution source for the album release will be any official streaming or download site, e.g. Apple Music or Spotify.

events.title: What will happen before GTA VI?
events.description: This is a market on predicting events that will occur before the release of Grand Theft Auto VI.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rockstar Games’ official delay of Grand Theft Auto VI to November 19, 2026, after missing earlier 2025 and spring 2026 targets, has extended the runway for cultural and tech milestones that traders are pricing into the market. Recent Take-Two earnings commentary and Rockstar statements emphasize polishing the open-world experience, reducing the likelihood of further slips while widening the window for high-profile releases. Markets show strong consensus around a Russia-Ukraine ceasefire materializing beforehand, alongside elevated odds for GPT-6 launching and new albums from Rihanna and Playboi Carti. Traders weigh historical release patterns, streaming chart trajectories, and AI development timelines against the fixed November deadline, with upcoming May earnings calls and potential trailer drops serving as near-term catalysts that could shift sentiment on which events clear the bar first.

markets.question: Will Harvey Weinstein be sentenced to no prison time?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the prison sentence, if any, imposed on Harvey Weinstein as part of his ongoing New York retrial (People of the State of New York v. Harvey Weinstein) by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve immediately based on the first sentence rendered in this case, regardless of any appeals. If Weinstein is found not guilty, there is a mistrial, or if the first sentencing does not include any jail or prison time, this market will resolve to "No Prison Time."

If no sentencing takes place by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will also resolve to "No Prison Time."

If the sentence falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

For the purposes of this market, it does not matter whether the sentence is concurrent or consecutive to any existing sentence Weinstein is already serving. The market will resolve based on the total prison sentence imposed in this case.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York court system or other involved U.S. government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent developments in Harvey Weinstein's legal proceedings, including a mistrial in his third New York rape trial on May 15, 2026, and stalled retrial outcomes, have reinforced trader consensus around "No Prison Time" as the leading outcome. Ongoing appeals of his 16-year California conviction and prior mixed New York verdicts, combined with the producer's health challenges and extended pretrial detention at Rikers, limit prospects for additional sentencing in the near term. The consecutive nature of existing terms and procedural setbacks further dampen expectations for significant new prison exposure, though unresolved charges and potential future resolutions introduce limited upside risk for shorter or mid-range terms.

events.title: 2026 NHL Stanley Cup Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NHL Stanley Cup championship.

eventMetadata.context_description: The tight clustering of 2026 Stanley Cup odds reflects the ongoing conference finals, where recent series results have elevated the Vegas Golden Knights and Carolina Hurricanes as the clear frontrunners. Vegas seized a 2-0 lead over the Colorado Avalanche in the West with strong road wins, while Carolina evened its Eastern matchup against the Montreal Canadiens at 1-1 following an overtime victory in Game 2. These developments have compressed probabilities between the top two contenders, underscoring their playoff momentum and home-ice potential in remaining games. The Canadiens and Avalanche trail due to their respective deficits and tougher paths through the bracket, though both retain realistic advancement chances given the best-of-seven format and historical comeback precedents in postseason hockey.

markets.question: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

events.title: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

eventMetadata.context_description: Xi Jinping’s entrenched authority as General Secretary of the Chinese Communist Party and Chairman of the Central Military Commission underpins the 93% trader consensus against removal before the end of 2026. Recent leadership adjustments through anti-corruption campaigns, including military personnel changes, have further centralized power without visible elite opposition or a designated successor. His active role in directing the 15th Five-Year Plan (2026-2030), high-level diplomatic engagements, and New Year messaging projecting continuity on core priorities signal institutional stability. Analysts highlight the absence of confirmed health issues or challenges ahead of the 21st Party Congress in late 2027, where any adjustments would likely preserve his position within the market’s resolution period.

markets.question: Will Gavin Newsom win the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gavin Newsom leads the trader consensus for the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination at 24.3% amid a wide-open field with no declared candidates. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez and Kamala Harris follow at 9.0% and 8.6%, reflecting their respective progressive base appeal and prior national profile, while senators and other governors trail further due to lower name recognition. Key differentiators include executive experience versus legislative records, fundraising capacity, and perceived general-election viability. Future consolidation will likely hinge on primary polling trends, endorsements, and formal announcements ahead of the 2028 cycle.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gavin Newsom leads the trader consensus for the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination at 24.3% amid a wide-open field with no declared candidates. His positioning stems from sustained national visibility as California governor, recent memoir promotion, and repeated media appearances that have kept him prominent in early speculation. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez and Kamala Harris follow at 9.0% and 8.6%, reflecting their respective progressive base appeal and prior national profile, while senators and other governors trail further due to lower name recognition. Key differentiators include executive experience versus legislative records, fundraising capacity, and perceived general-election viability. Future consolidation will likely hinge on primary polling trends, endorsements, and formal announcements ahead of the 2028 cycle.
